long_description:
Adapter Kind: Adapter Instance: Infrastructure Health Adapter - vRealize Operations Collector-vrops-data01 - Collecting - Data receiving vRealizeOpsMgrAPI (vRealize Operations Collector-vrops-master) - Collecting - Data receiving VMWARE - torm001-vc.m001.torpr.dir - Collecting - Data receiving SDDC Health Adapter Instance - 4 - Collecting - Data receiving torm001-vc.m001.torpr.dir - Collecting - Data receiving Alert Description: A vSphere HA master agent considers a host to have failed if it loses contact with the vSphere HA agent on the host, the host does not respond to pings on any of the management interfaces, and the master does not observe any datastore heartbeats. This problem can occur when a computer on the network is configured to have the same IP address as one of the ESX/ESXi hosts in a HA cluster. In this situation, the HA agent receives invalid data and generates errors. The HA agent does not function properly until it is reconfigured. The frequency of this problem depends on how often the IP address conflict occurs. Alert ID: 6b8d3576-0789-497d-bcc4-d89b494de508 Alert Impact: health Alert Name: vSphere High Availability (HA) has detected a possible host failure - IBM Alert Subtype: Availability Alert Type: Virtualization/Hypervisor Cancel Date: Unavailable Control State: Open Criticality: critical Host Name: vrops-replica.torpr.dir Alert Link: https://172.25.2.51/vcf-operations/ui/inventory;mode=hierarchy;resourceId=218c9449-a5ae-415d-9656-743acb46cfa9;tab=alerts-and-symptoms;subTab=alerts;entityId=6b8d3576-0789-497d-bcc4-d89b494de508 Notification Rule Description: Notification Rule Name: PD: vSphere High Availability (HA) has detected a possible host failure Object Efficiency State: 1.0 Object Health State: 4.0 Object Risk State: 1.0 Product: ['N/A', 'N/A', 'N/A'] Recommendations: Runbook: https://pages.github.ibm.com/cloudlab/internal-docs/vrops_sev2_alerts.html#vsphere-high-availability-has-detected-a-possible-host-failure | Find the computer that has the duplicate IP address and reconfigure it to have a different IP address. This fault will be cleared and the alert canceled when the underlying problem is resolved and the vSphere HA master agent is able to connect to the HA agent on the host.

NOTE: You can use the Duplicate IP warning in the /var/log/vmkernel log file on an ESX host or the /var/log/messages log file on an ESXi host to identify the computer that has the duplicate IP address. Runbook URL: https://pages.github.ibm.com/cloudlab/internal-docs/vrops_sev2_alerts.html#vsphere-high-availability-has-detected-a-possible-host-failure Resource ID: 218c9449-a5ae-415d-9656-743acb46cfa9 Resource Kind: HostSystem Resource Name: m001t01esx001.m001.torpr.dir Status: Active Symptoms: Symptom Set - self Symptoms vSphere HA detected a host failure | m001t01esx001.m001.torpr.dir | 218c9449-a5ae-415d-9656-743acb46cfa9 : vSphere HA detected a possible host failure of host m001t01esx001.m001.torpr.dir in cluster mgmt-stretch01-witness in datacenter IBMCloud Recommendations: Runbook: https://pages.github.ibm.com/cloudlab/internal-docs/vrops_sev2_alerts.html#vsphere-high-availability-has-detected-a-possible-host-failure | Find the computer that has the duplicate IP address and reconfigure it to have a different IP address. This fault will be cleared and the alert canceled when the underlying problem is resolved and the vSphere HA master agent is able to connect to the HA agent on the host.

NOTE: You can use the Duplicate IP warning in the /var/log/vmkernel log file on an ESX host or the /var/log/messages log file on an ESXi host to identify the computer that has the duplicate IP address.
